text.title: DDD Aggregates Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce an aggregate with invariants + events + tests.

step.name: identify-boundary
step.action: Group entities/value objects under one root such that every invariant is enforceable inside one transaction. Mark child-entity count; if > 5, split.
step.decision-gate: If invariants span aggregates, FAIL and redesign with eventual consistency.

step.name: root-and-invariants
step.action: Define the root class with no public setters. Encode each invariant as a precondition inside a command method (raise `DomainError` on violation).

step.name: command-methods
step.action: For each state-changing use case, write a named command method (`place`, `add_item`, `cancel`). Each method mutates state via private setters, then appends to `_events`.

step.name: cross-aggregate-refs
step.action: Replace any object refs with identity (`customer_id: UUID`). Provide accessors that return the ID, not the foreign aggregate.

step.name: invariant-tests
step.action: Write a failing test per invariant. Name them `test_cannot_*` / `test_must_*`. Tests instantiate the aggregate, call the command method, assert raised exception or state.
step.decision-gate: If a test passes immediately (no implementation), the invariant is not actually checked — fix the implementation.
